intelligence.hypothesis: Insufficient telemetry; awaiting next valid capture.

vision_report.compositional_truth_check: The registry is largely invalidated by current visual evidence. P1 (String of Nickels), P2 (Mexican Mint), and P4 (Silver Guest) are not visible or are indistinguishable from bare soil/debris. P3 (Pothos) is the only identifiable specimen, though it has suffered significant structural degradation.

inventory_reconciliation.P3: Degraded (Pothos leaf present, but anchor rabbit is obscured/missing in later frames)

plant_audit.P1: No plant matter detected in yellow pot.
plant_audit.P2: No plant matter detected in black pot.
plant_audit.P3: Pothos leaf visible in early frames, showing signs of chlorosis and wilting. By T-1, the specimen appears to have collapsed or been removed.

biome_observations.soil_condition: Soil appears consistently dark and potentially waterlogged or devoid of organic life. No signs of new growth.
biome_observations.surface_debris: Presence of crushed eggshells (calcium amendment) noted in the black pot. No fungal growth observed, but lighting conditions are poor.
biome_observations.desk_surface: Clean, no significant organic debris outside of pots.

temporal_deltas.EARLIEST_to_T-4: Minimal change; Pothos leaf remains turgid.
temporal_deltas.T-4_to_T-3: Pothos leaf shows slight postural drooping.
temporal_deltas.T-3_to_T-2: Pothos leaf exhibits significant wilting; stem curvature indicates loss of turgor pressure.
temporal_deltas.T-2_to_T-1: Total disappearance of the Pothos specimen; pot appears empty.
temporal_deltas.T-1_to_CURRENT: No change; system remains in a state of total loss.

vision_report.visual_health_inference: The biome is in a state of terminal decline. The Pothos (P3) has likely succumbed to root rot or severe dehydration, leading to its removal or total collapse. The other pots (P1, P2, P4) show no evidence of the registered species, suggesting either total mortality or that they were never successfully established.
vision_report.anomalies: The presence of eggshells suggests an attempt at soil amendment, but the lack of plant survival indicates this was insufficient to save the specimens.
vision_report.narrative_description: The audit reveals a catastrophic failure of the indoor botanical setup. Starting from a state where only one specimen (Pothos) was clearly visible, the sequence documents a rapid decline in health followed by the total disappearance of all plant life. The environment is currently devoid of viable vegetation.
